_comment: Registry of every stateful instrument in the lab and its DECLARED relationship to time. lag_report.py reads this to tell deliberate freezes apart from silent drift. Add a row here whenever you build a new stateful instrument; an unregistered store is, by policy, a drift waiting to be discovered late.

instruments.reason: General session mirror deliberately frozen 2026-05-02 (persistence-layer README status note); superseded by narrower instruments. Guarded by context_card --check-stale since 2026-06-15.
Superseded by pull_graph_v1 on 2026-05-30; v0 retained as history, not maintained.

Caught up each weekly lab session (snapshot 5 -> front 116 on 2026-06-29). NOTE 2026-06-29: the IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES 'one snapshot per newly-landed essay / continuous-read' cadence is a fiction in a weekly lab — nothing runs the snapshot between sessions, so it WILL drift to ~essays-landed-per-week between checks. It drifted to 8-behind only a week after the 084->109 catch-up. So 'live' here operationally means 'lag 0 at each lab session', and max_lag is set to one week's realistic essay yield (8), not the aspirational 3. The lag report detects; remediation is still manual and must happen in-session.
Weekly reread of a frozen 5-essay sample. Recency-based, not frontier-based: it should be READ on cadence, not extended.
